title: pelexia sagittata
side_effects: Pelexia sagittata can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and eye problems. Ingesting the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and stomach pain due to the toxic compounds present in it. Overuse or prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can lead to headaches, dizziness, and respiratory issues in some individuals. Furthermore, improper handling of the plant may also trigger skin conditions like eczema or dermatitis in sensitive individuals.
